description: A wide flat-design FEMA anti-fraud public-awareness graphic on a white background. On the left is a large blue rounded-triangle warning sign containing a bold exclamation mark; a small flat illustration of a woman with long dark hair, in a dark top and blue jeans, sits casually on the triangle's lower edge while using a laptop. On the right, a large dark-navy bold headline reads 'Beware of Fraud and Scams,' followed by smaller blue body text: 'After a disaster, scam artists often attempt to take advantage of disaster survivors. We encourage survivors to watch for and report any suspicious activity.' Below a thin divider, a bold heading 'Ways to Report Fraud' is followed by contact lines in blue: 'Email: StopFEMAFraud@fema.dhs.gov / Call: 1-866-223-0814 / Fax: 202-212-4926.' At the bottom: 'For more information visit fema.gov/disaster-fraud' and the DHS seal with the 'FEMA' wordmark at bottom-right. Visual style: clean flat-vector government infographic in FEMA/DHS blues with friendly illustration and clear typographic hierarchy. Messaging technique: cautionary consumer-protection PSA providing actionable reporting channels for International Fraud Awareness Week. Target audience: disaster survivors and the general public.
notable_text: Beware of Fraud and Scams | Ways to Report Fraud — Email: StopFEMAFraud@fema.dhs.gov / Call: 1-866-223-0814 / Fax: 202-212-4926 | fema.gov/disaster-fraud
